chapter: Chapter: Tribals, Dikus and the Vision of a Golden Age

questions.question: Why were forests important to tribal communities?

options.A: They provided food, shelter and livelihood
options.B: They were used for building factories
options.C: They were used for building railways
options.D: They were only used for hunting animals

questions.explanation: Forests provided food, fuel, shelter and other resources essential for tribal life.

questions.question: What was shifting cultivation practiced by tribal communities?

options.A: Growing crops on one piece of land permanently
options.B: Moving from one place to another for farming

questions.explanation: In shifting cultivation, tribals moved from one place to another after cultivating land for a short time.

questions.question: Why did traders and moneylenders enter tribal areas during British rule?

options.A: To exploit tribal resources and labour

questions.explanation: Traders and moneylenders entered tribal areas mainly to exploit forest resources and tribal labour.

questions.question: Which activity was restricted by British forest laws?
questions.explanation: British forest laws restricted shifting cultivation and access to forest resources.

questions.question: Which tribal leader started a movement against British exploitation in the late nineteenth century?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda led a major tribal movement against British rule and exploitation.

questions.question: What did Birsa Munda encourage tribal people to do?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda encouraged tribals to resist British rule and exploitation.

questions.question: What was the meaning of Birsa Munda's 'Golden Age'?

options.B: A time when tribals would regain their rights and land

questions.explanation: Birsa Munda believed in restoring a Golden Age where tribal communities would regain their land and freedom.

questions.question: Which tribe was associated with Birsa Munda?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda belonged to the Munda tribal community.

questions.question: What was the main aim of tribal rebellions during British rule?

options.B: To restore traditional rights over land and forests

questions.explanation: Tribal rebellions aimed to regain traditional land and forest rights.

questions.question: How did moneylenders exploit tribal communities?

options.A: By giving loans with very high interest

questions.explanation: Moneylenders charged high interest and trapped tribals in debt.

questions.question: Why did many tribals lose their land during colonial rule?

options.A: Due to debt and new land revenue systems

questions.explanation: Many tribals lost their land due to debt and colonial land revenue systems.

questions.question: Which colonial policy affected tribal access to forest resources?
questions.explanation: Forest laws limited tribal access to forests.

questions.question: What type of economy did most tribal communities follow?
questions.explanation: Most tribal communities depended on forest resources for their livelihood.

questions.question: Which colonial activity increased exploitation of tribal areas?
questions.explanation: Colonial forest reservation policies limited tribal access to forest resources.

questions.question: What type of movement was led by Birsa Munda?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda's movement had both religious and political aspects.

questions.question: What was the main impact of British forest policies on tribals?
questions.explanation: British forest policies restricted traditional tribal activities such as hunting and shifting cultivation.

questions.question: Which natural resource formed the basis of tribal livelihood?
questions.explanation: Forests were central to the livelihood of tribal communities.

questions.question: Which group of people did tribals call 'Dikus'?
questions.explanation: Tribals used the term 'Diku' for outsiders who exploited them.

questions.question: What was the main reason for tribal revolts in the nineteenth century?

options.A: Loss of land and forest rights

questions.explanation: Tribal revolts were mainly due to loss of land and restrictions on forest use.

questions.question: Which colonial activity led to deforestation and tribal displacement?
questions.explanation: Commercial forestry policies caused deforestation and displaced many tribal communities.

questions.question: What role did tribal leaders play during rebellions?
questions.explanation: Tribal leaders organized resistance movements against colonial exploitation.

questions.question: Which economic activity caused tribals to fall into debt?
questions.explanation: High-interest loans from moneylenders caused tribals to fall into debt.

questions.question: Which colonial change disrupted traditional tribal society?

options.A: Introduction of new land revenue systems

questions.explanation: New land revenue systems disrupted traditional tribal land ownership.

questions.question: Which idea inspired tribals to unite under Birsa Munda?
questions.explanation: Birsa Munda's idea of a Golden Age inspired tribals to unite.

questions.question: What was the main outcome of tribal resistance movements?
questions.explanation: Tribal resistance movements highlighted the need to protect tribal rights and resources.
